name: goose (Go Database Migration Tool)

agent_readiness.notes: Go database migration tool supporting SQL and Go migrations. goose -dir migrations postgres 'postgres://user:pass@localhost/db' up. Migration files: 001_create_users.sql with -- +goose Up / -- +goose Down. Go migrations: func init() { goose.AddMigration(upCreateUsers, downCreateUsers) }. func upCreateUsers(tx *sql.Tx) error { ... }. goose create create_users sql → new migration file. goose up → apply all pending. goose up-by-one → apply next. goose down → rollback. goose status. goose version. goose fix → convert old timestamp naming. Embedded migrations: goose.SetBaseFS(migrationFiles). Supports: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, MSSQL, TiDB, ClickHouse, Vertica. Versioned: timestamp or sequential. Library usage: goose.Up(db, './migrations').

gotchas: SQL and Go migration types — choose one style consistently
Timestamp vs sequential versioning — pick at project start
goose fix needed when switching from old timestamp format
go-pg users: use golang-migrate or dbmate instead
best_when: Go projects needing database migrations with SQL or programmatic Go migrations
When embedding migrations in Go binary is needed (FS embedding)
Teams wanting CLI and library API for the same tool
avoid_when: When atlas's schema-based approach is preferred over sequential migrations
SQLite-only projects where lite alternatives exist
summary: goose is a Go database migration tool. SQL files with -- +goose Up / Down or Go migration functions. goose up/down/status/create. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, ClickHouse, MSSQL. Embed migrations with Go FS. Library API: goose.Up(db, dir). MIT by pressly. Best Go-native migration tool with both CLI and library support. Timestamp or sequential versioning. Go migrations for complex logic.
